candidate: standalone Triton compact variable-row grouped GEMM tile probe

implementation_notes.fixed_probe_bug: The first full-size attempt faulted because the Triton B operand offset used 32-bit element arithmetic for expert * K * N. The probe now casts the expert base offset to int64 before full-size runs.
implementation_notes.read: The compact row count saves about 31.9% of rows versus global max padding, but this naive Triton grouped-GEMM schedule loses more than 2x to rocBLAS/hipBLASLt-backed padded torch.bmm(out=) on both dgrad-style contractions.

next_action: Do not integrate this naive Triton compact grouped GEMM into the full layer. Keep the retained compact-reduce padded torch.bmm path as the baseline; future compact work needs a library/CK/TileLang schedule that reaches high MFMA utilization for tiny-M grouped GEMMs, plus wgrad and compact reduce kernels.
